Simplify \(\dfrac{\sqrt{b}}{\sqrt{a}-\sqrt{b}}+\dfrac{\sqrt{a}}{\sqrt{a}+\sqrt{b}}\).
A. \(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{a}-\sqrt{b}}\)
B. \(\dfrac{a+2\sqrt{ab}-b}{a-b}\)
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{b}+\sqrt{a}}{2\sqrt{a}}\)
D. \(\dfrac{b+2\sqrt{ab}-a}{a-b}\)
E. \(\dfrac{a+b}{a-b}\)

Find the greatest value of \(3x+2y\) if \((x,y)\) is a point lying in the region \(OABCD\) (including the boundary).

A. 15
B. 13
C. 12
D. 9
E. 8

The diagram shows the graphs of \(y=ax^2+bx\) and \(y=cx+d\). The solutions of the equation \(ax^2+bx=cx+d\) are

A. \(-1, 1\).
B. \(-1, 2\).
C. \(0, 1\).
D. \(0, 3\).
E. \(1, 3\).

If \(\log(p+q)=\log p+\log q\), then
A. \(p=q=1\).
B. \(p=\dfrac{q}{q-1}\).
C. \(p=\dfrac{q}{q+1}\).
D. \(p=\dfrac{q+1}{q}\).
E. \(p=\dfrac{q-1}{q}\).

The expression \(x^2-2x+k\) is divisible by \((x+1)\). Find the remainder when it is divided by \((x+3)\).
A. 1
B. 4
C. 12
D. 16
E. 18

Find the H.C.F. and L.C.M. of \(ab^2c\) and \(abc^3\)
A. H.C.F. \(a\), L.C.M. \(a^2b^3c^4\).
B. H.C.F. \(abc\), L.C.M. \(ab^2c^3\).
C. H.C.F. \(abc\), L.C.M. \(a^2b^3c^4\).
D. H.C.F. \(ab^2c^3\), L.C.M. \(abc\).
E. H.C.F. \(a^2b^3c^4\), L.C.M. \(abc\).

If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are the roots of the quadratic equation \(x^2-3x-1=0\), find the value of \(\dfrac{1}{\alpha}+\dfrac{1}{\beta}\).
A. \(-3\)
B. \(-1\)
C. \(-\dfrac{1}{3}\)
D. \(\dfrac{2}{3}\)
E. 3

If the simultaneous equations \(\begin{cases} y=x^2-k \\ y=x \end{cases}\) have only one solution, find \(k\).
A. \(-1\)
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{4}\)
C. \(-4\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\)
E. 1

The price of a cylindrical cake of radius \(r \text{ cm}\) and height \(h \text{ cm}\) varies directly as the volume. If \(r=5 \text{ cm}\) and \(h=4 \text{ cm}\), the price is \(\$30\). Find the price when \(r=4 \text{ cm}\) and \(h=6 \text{ cm}\).

A. \(\$25\)
B. \(\$28.80\)
C. \(\$31.50\)
D. \(\$36\)
E. \(\$54\)

A. \(24:\pi\)
B. \(3:1\)
C. \(6:\pi\)

The figure shows a solid consisting of a cylinder of height \(h\) and a hemisphere of radius \(r\). The area of the curved surface of the cylinder is twice that of the hemisphere. Find the ratio volume of cylinder : volume of hemisphere.

A. \(1:3\)
B. \(2:3\)
C. \(3:4\)
D. \(3:2\)
E. \(3:1\)

A merchant marks his goods \(25\%\) above the cost. He allows \(10\%\) discount on the marked price for a cash sale. Find the percentage profit the merchant makes for a cash sale.
A. \(12.5\%\)
B. \(15\%\)
C. \(22.5\%\)
D. \(35\%\)
E. \(37.5\%\)

B. \(\cos\theta\).
C. \(\tan\theta\).
D. \(\dfrac{1}{\sin\theta}\).

B. 1.

Given that the positive numbers \(p, q, r, s\) are in G.S., which of the following must be true?
I. \(kp, kq, kr, ks\) are in G.S., where \(k\) is a non-zero constant.
II. \(a^p, a^q, a^r, a^s\) are in G.S., where \(a\) is a positive constant.
III. \(\log p, \log q, \log r, \log s\) are in A.S.
A. I only
B. II only

B. \(\sqrt{34} \text{ cm}\)
C. \(7 \text{ cm}\)

In factorizing the expression \(a^4+a^2b^2+b^4\), we find that
A. \((a^2-b^2)\) is a factor.
B. \((a^2+b^2)\) is a factor.
C. \((a^2-ab-b^2)\) is a factor.
D. \((a^2-ab+b^2)\) is a factor.
E. it cannot be factorized.

B. \(a=-5, c=2\).
C. \(a=5, c=-2\).
D. \(a=1, c=-2\).
E. \(a=-1, c=2\).

Which of the following gives the compound interest on \(\$10\,000\) at \(6\% \text{ p.a.}\) for one year, compounded monthly?
A. \(\$10\,000 \times \dfrac{0.06}{12} \times 12\)
B. \(\$10\,000(1.06^{12}-1)\)
C. \(\$10\,000\left(1+\dfrac{0.06}{12}\right)^{12}\)
D. \(\$10\,000\left[\left(1+\dfrac{0.06}{12}\right)^{12}-1\right]\)
E. \(\$10\,000\left[\left(1+\dfrac{0.6}{12}\right)^{12}-1\right]\)
